mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding real output and corporate earnings, directly supporting equity prices over the subsequent month as investors price in higher future cash flows.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC between INDPRO change and 20-day forward return stays below 0.05, or positive returns occur less than 55% of the time when INDPRO is above its 12-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below the 6.0% threshold, it signals a tightening labor market that compresses profit margins through wage inflation and raises the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, both of which weigh on equities.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the average return following triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate near zero and CPI rising, real interest rates become increasingly negative, encouraging speculative asset purchases and raising the risk of an inflation-driven correction when the Fed eventually normalizes policy, producing a negative forward return.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC of the FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction term (product of normalized values) falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in > 60% of months when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts recovering from crisis lows fuel residential investment, construction employment, and consumer wealth effects, leading to above-average equity returns over the next quarter during the housing recovery regime.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in the housing recovery regime (HOUST rising from trough) are not significantly higher than all other periods (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC over the 2012-2014 window is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in nonfarm payrolls reflect broad economic strength and rising aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings and pushing equities higher over short weekly horizons as labor market data is released.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following higher-than-expected payrolls releases are negative more than 50% of the time, or the average excess return after releases with payroll gains > 150k is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1% annualized signals systemic demand weakness that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered quarters, or the signal produces an information ratio below 0.2 over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and residential investment, a powerful combined driver of GDP that consistently lifts equities over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the bivariate signal (both proxies above trigger) falls below 0.04 in any 3-year window, or the hit rate for positive 20-day returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation, measured by a CPI index level above the long-term trend, erodes real earnings and forces the Fed to consider rate hikes, creating a persistent headwind for equities that becomes dominant when CPI surpasses the 3% YoY threshold.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months, or the average excess return during high-inflation periods is not significantly negative (z-test p > 0.10).
